jokes.text: Maybe you should jump up into midair and do it... If you jump into the air and grab a live wire... you won't get electrocuted. But then if you land on the ground and you're still holding that wire... you'll be blown to bits. I saw it in Tango and Cash.
jokes.setup: Mac confidently explains electrical safety based on action movie logic
jokes.punchline: He casually admits his electrical expertise comes from Tango and Cash
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - confidently stupid, mixing pseudo-science with action movie references. The absurd escalation from grounding to aerial acrobatics is excellent.

jokes.text: So I'm supposed to risk my life based on something... that you saw in the movie Tango and Cash?
jokes.setup: Mac's elaborate electrical safety theory
jokes.punchline: Dennis's incredulous reaction highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Dennis as the straight man perfectly puncturing Mac's confidence. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Dennis, get me a harness 'cause I'll have to be swinging in the air to do this.
jokes.setup: Frank decides to fix the electrical problem himself
jokes.punchline: He commits to Mac's ridiculous aerial wire-grabbing theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Frank's equally poor decision-making. Escalates the stupidity naturally.

jokes.text: They fell off the back of a truck.
jokes.setup: Mac proudly shows off free speakers
jokes.punchline: Uses the classic mob euphemism literally and cluelessly
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for the coming mob storyline. Mac's obliviousness to criminal implications is in character.

jokes.text: We don't have any electricity! That's why we needed the flashlight.
jokes.setup: Mac excited about plugging in speakers
jokes.punchline: Pointing out they have no power due to earlier events
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the electrical disaster, solid continuity joke

jokes.text: That's a... big bag of drugs.
jokes.setup: The gang discovers drugs in the speakers
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact observation of obvious danger
jokes.explanation: Simple understatement works for the moment but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: But, like, what's the worst that could happen?
jokes.setup: Mac acknowledges someone will be looking for the drugs
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with famous last words
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony setup. Predictable but well-executed for the genre.

jokes.text: The elaborate disposal debate - kids with shovels, fume situations, solving society's drug problem
jokes.setup: Simple problem of disposing drugs
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous objections to every solution
jokes.explanation: Great escalating sequence that parodies overthinking simple problems. Good ensemble work.

jokes.text: We're saying we're gonna do the drugs... and then we're gonna try and fix all the lights.
jokes.setup: Complex plan to sell drugs for money
jokes.punchline: Charlie thinks they're going to consume the drugs for energy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his unique logic leads to hilariously wrong conclusion. Excellent character-specific humor.

jokes.text: That's asinine... Your way's terrible.
jokes.setup: Charlie's ridiculous drug consumption plan
jokes.punchline: Dee's brutal dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Charlie's absurdity. Dee's bluntness works well here.

jokes.text: Back in my day, only the biggest pieces of shit dealt drugs.
jokes.setup: Frank objects to the gang's drug dealing plan
jokes.punchline: Frank Reynolds calling anyone else a piece of shit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank hypocrisy - the most morally corrupt character taking the high ground. Strong character work.

jokes.text: Hey, Bingo. Frank sent us.
jokes.setup: Frank explicitly tells them not to mention his name
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately mentions Frank's name
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with perfect timing. Mac's obliviousness drives the joke effectively.

jokes.text: Frank, huh? I'm gonna skin that son of a bitch and wear his face.
jokes.setup: Bingo learns Frank sent them
jokes.punchline: Casually threatens extreme violence then continues normally
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor escalation. The casual delivery of extreme threat followed by 'Anyway, what's up?' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: We got a bucket of nose clams fresh from the sea... Sweet, delicious nose clams that are looking for a home
jokes.setup: Mac tries to use coded language for drugs
jokes.punchline: Creates increasingly ridiculous euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd euphemism that becomes increasingly elaborate. 'Nose clams' is wonderfully ridiculous and quotable.

jokes.text: These are the kind of nose clams that make you want to dance the night away.
jokes.setup: Mac continues his nose clam metaphor
jokes.punchline: Adds dancing element to the already absurd drug euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the nose clam concept. Mac's commitment to the bit makes it funnier.

jokes.text: These are the kind of nose clams that you crush up into a line of white powder... They come from Colombia. They're illegal. And they rhyme with 'propane.'
jokes.setup: Mac's elaborate coded language completely fails
jokes.punchline: He gives up and describes cocaine in explicit detail while maintaining the clam metaphor
jokes.explanation: Fantastic payoff to the nose clam setup. The progression from subtle to hilariously explicit while maintaining the metaphor is brilliant.

jokes.text: Perfect. You found a perfect medium ground there... Why didn't you just come out and say that? Instead of dancing around it. That is so tacky.
jokes.setup: Mac's overly elaborate drug euphemisms
jokes.punchline: The drug dealer lectures them on proper etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal where the criminal has better social skills than the protagonists. Bingo's professionalism is funny.

jokes.text: That is the kindergarten behavior of drug dealing.
jokes.setup: Bingo criticizes their approach
jokes.punchline: Uses children's behavior as drug dealing critique
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of kindergarten behavior with drug dealing. Good character voice for Bingo.

jokes.text: The certain someone/certain something speech
jokes.setup: Gangsters try to sound threatening
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate euphemistic language becomes incomprehensible
jokes.explanation: Good concept but runs a bit long. The overwrought euphemistic language parodies gangster movie clichés well.

jokes.text: Fellas, we're about six beers' deep. So I think you're gonna have to... be a little bit more clear
jokes.setup: Incomprehensible gangster speech
jokes.punchline: Mac honestly admits he's too drunk to follow threats to his life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac response - prioritizes his drunk state over mortal danger. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, Friday is the day... that we may or may not be forced... to chop your limbs off... and distribute them evenly amongst your friends and your family.
jokes.setup: Explaining consequences of not paying
jokes.punchline: Describes horrific violence with bureaucratic politeness and euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor mixing extreme violence with polite uncertainty ('may or may not'). The equal distribution detail is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Well, Friday is the day that we may or may not be forced to chop your limbs off and distribute them evenly amongst your friends and your family.
jokes.setup: Question about what happens Friday
jokes.punchline: Extremely detailed violent threat delivered with unnecessary specificity
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with absurd level of organizational detail

jokes.text: You guys, those drugs were worth 25 grand!
jokes.setup: Mac's excitement over $300
jokes.punchline: Revealing they lost tens of thousands of dollars
jokes.explanation: Strong dramatic escalation showing how badly they screwed up

jokes.text: You guys, those drugs were worth 25 grand!... Oh, dude... a one, a two... a $300!
jokes.setup: Revelation that they severely undersold valuable drugs
jokes.punchline: Charlie's excited counting reveals the pathetic sum
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their incompetence. Charlie's proud counting makes the failure more comedic.

jokes.text: I was using 'Dead Presidents' as a cover... He said to the man he wanted many, many thousands of green people... From history times.
jokes.setup: Charlie claims he asked for more money using code
jokes.punchline: His 'code' was incomprehensible nonsense about historical green people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie moment. His version of street smart code is perfectly illogical. 'History times' is wonderfully Charlie-esque.

jokes.text: I was using 'Dead Presidents' as a cover. You didn't get that? He said to the man he wanted many, many thousands of green people from history times.
jokes.setup: Mac claiming he asked for more money
jokes.punchline: Charlie's interpretation of Mac's terrible code words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie translation of Mac's failed attempt at slang

jokes.text: We are going to get whacked off by a bunch of scary Italian guys... They implied they wanted to whack us all off!
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to describe mob threats
jokes.punchline: Accidentally uses sexual euphemism instead of murder terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic malapropism that works for Dennis's character. Slightly predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: What can we get for 300 then? Uh, you want an exotic bird?
jokes.setup: They need drugs to resell but only have $300
jokes.punchline: Drug dealer offers completely random exotic bird
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected offer that highlights the absurd world they're in. Random but fits the show's logic.

jokes.text: How do three men in their 30s not have $800 between them? They're... The economy is in shambles... The NASDAQ! Dow Jones! It's up then down.
jokes.setup: Dennis points out their pathetic financial situation
jokes.punchline: Mac blames complex economic forces for their personal failures
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's tendency to externalize blame. The vague financial terminology adds to the humor.

jokes.text: The economy is in shambles. Have you taken a close look at the marketplace right now? The NASDAQ! Dow Jones! It's up then down.
jokes.setup: Question about their poverty
jokes.punchline: Mac blaming global economics for their personal failures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac deflection of personal responsibility to larger forces

jokes.text: The economy is in shambles. Have you taken a close look at the marketplace right now? The NASDAQ! Dow Jones! It's up then down.
jokes.setup: Defending their poverty
jokes.punchline: Charlie pretending to understand economics with nonsense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - using big words he doesn't understand. 'Up then down' shows his economic ignorance.

jokes.text: You are gonna get us into that country club... And we're gonna sell the pills there... You can't make no $25,000 from that amount of pills... You're gonna have to turn a trick or two.
jokes.setup: Plan to sell pills at country club
jokes.punchline: Drug dealer casually suggests prostitution as additional income
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that introduces the prostitution subplot. Bingo's matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: I'll do it... Charlie, you're not quite cut from the right cloth... Mac, you're too low class... I was thinking about Dennis.
jokes.setup: Discussion of who should be the prostitute
jokes.punchline: Frank systematically dismisses Charlie and Mac for different character flaws
jokes.explanation: Good character work where Frank's assessments are brutally accurate. Each dismissal fits the character perfectly.

jokes.text: Holy shit. Did you guys see that midget dressed like a lawn jockey? That's a jockey, Charlie... That's a real jockey. The ones that ride on the horses...
jokes.setup: Charlie spots what he thinks is decorative figure
jokes.punchline: Reveals Charlie doesn't understand what actual jockeys are
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie ignorance moment. His confusion about basic things is a great character trait that feels earned.

jokes.text: That's a jockey, Charlie. That's a real jockey. The ones that ride on the horses...
jokes.setup: Charlie calling jockey a lawn ornament
jokes.punchline: Mac's patient explanation of what jockeys are
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie confusion moment, shows his disconnect from reality

jokes.text: Holy shit. You can talk? I would've figured if anything, your voice would be like super high.
jokes.setup: Charlie meets a jockey
jokes.punchline: Expresses shock that the person can talk and makes assumptions about their voice
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Charlie's jockey confusion. His genuine surprise at basic human abilities is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Holy shit. You can talk?
jokes.setup: Jockey speaking to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie's shock that person can speak
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - treating person like an animal

jokes.text: I would've figured if anything, your voice would be like super high.
jokes.setup: Charlie's surprise at jockey talking
jokes.punchline: His specific expectations about their voice
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Charlie's dehumanizing assumptions

jokes.text: Now, there's only one thing that these rich old men understand. And that is money... And you are going to be my secretary, Linda.
jokes.setup: Mac explains their country club con strategy
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines Dee by making her subservient secretary
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the coming role-play disaster. Mac's sexism is consistent with character.

jokes.text: Jameson is investing in new technologies... Lasers. Lasers!... Lasers, Linda!
jokes.setup: Mac tries to sound like sophisticated businessman
jokes.punchline: His only business idea is 'lasers' which he repeats with increasing desperation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Mac moment showing his shallow understanding of business. The repetition and escalation work perfectly.

jokes.text: Who wants to buy some drugs?
jokes.setup: Elaborate business roleplay setup
jokes.punchline: Dee abandons all pretense and asks directly about drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Mac's elaborate scheme. Dee's directness cuts through all the nonsense effectively.

jokes.text: You could make a killing here, bro... Really? I wouldn't have to bang these ladies? No... it's all fancy dinners, exotic vacations.
jokes.setup: Dennis learns about upscale escort work
jokes.punchline: His main concern is avoiding actual sex, revealing his vanity priorities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis prioritizing his image over necessity. His vanity drives the humor.

jokes.text: Ah, you know what they say. Nobody parties like a jockey! I was not familiar with that expression, Buster.
jokes.setup: Charlie sells pills to jockeys successfully
jokes.punchline: Buster makes up saying that doesn't exist, Charlie admits ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Charlie's honest admission of not knowing a fake saying. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: I think maybe... I was a centaur in my past life... Well, something tells me you probably were never half-man, half-horse.
jokes.setup: Charlie theorizes about his connection to horses
jokes.punchline: Buster's gentle but firm reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic leap to mythological creatures. Buster's deadpan response grounds the absurdity nicely.

jokes.text: So come on, you old son of a gun... and-and let Buster do a line off your boner. No?
jokes.setup: Buster seems friendly and helpful
jokes.punchline: Casually propositions Charlie for drug use involving genitals
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that reveals Buster's true nature. The casual delivery makes it more disturbing/funny.

jokes.text: So come on, you old son of a gun and let Buster do a line off your boner.
jokes.setup: Charlie talking to the horse named Buster
jokes.punchline: Offering sexual favor involving drug use
jokes.explanation: Shockingly inappropriate but perfectly Charlie in its wrongness

jokes.text: Dude, I think I was a centaur in my past life... You smell like cheese... It's horse shit... The horse was eating a lot of cheese.
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to explain his horse connection while covered in manure
jokes.punchline: His explanation for cheese smell involves horses eating cheese
jokes.explanation: Good callback to centaur theory with typical Charlie logic about horses eating cheese. Character consistency is strong.

jokes.text: Dude, I think I was a centaur in my past life. It totally occurred to me.
jokes.setup: Charlie's earlier centaur theory
jokes.punchline: Continuing to earnestly discuss it
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Charlie's persistence with absurd ideas

jokes.text: Don't talk to me that way. Don't talk to me. I am your pimp. You are my ho.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks Frank to leave
jokes.punchline: Frank asserts dominance using pimp terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment establishing the new power dynamic. His bluntness about their relationship is characteristic.

jokes.text: Dennis is a prostitute now... No, I'm not prostitute, okay? Yes, he is... There's no banging old ladies and dudes.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to maintain dignity about his new job
jokes.punchline: His protests reveal he's already committed to the role despite denials
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict showing Dennis's vanity conflicting with necessity. The contradiction is well-played.

jokes.text: You're supposed to walk like the rest of the Americans. You walk, you know? On their two feet!
jokes.setup: Mac asking how to get home
jokes.punchline: Frank's condescending explanation of walking
jokes.explanation: Good Frank callousness with unnecessary explanation

jokes.text: How am I supposed to get home? You're supposed to walk like the rest of the Americans. You walk, you know? On their two feet!
jokes.setup: Frank is left behind without transportation
jokes.punchline: Mac's patronizing explanation of walking as if Frank is foreign
jokes.explanation: Good Mac moment showing his condescending nature. The 'rest of the Americans' phrasing is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: I did have absolutely nothing to do with it... You did or you didn't? I did... You did have something to do with it? No. No. I did have absolutely nothing to do with it.
jokes.setup: Frank tries to deny involvement using double negatives
jokes.punchline: Creates confusion that makes him sound guilty while trying to sound innocent
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion showing Frank's verbal incompetence under pressure. The grammar confusion is well-executed.

jokes.text: You want to join our crew, huh?... Okay, first things first. You gotta prove your loyalty... I want you... to go clean the bathrooms.
jokes.setup: Frank wants to join the mob
jokes.punchline: First loyalty test is mundane bathroom cleaning
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from expected mob initiation to mundane chores. Subverts gangster movie expectations nicely.

jokes.text: I'm gonna make him our bitch till Friday... And after that? I wanna whack him off irregardless.
jokes.setup: Gangster explains plan for Frank
jokes.punchline: Uses sexual euphemism instead of murder term, plus says 'irregardless'
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier euphemism confusion, plus the 'irregardless' mistake adds to character. Decent but predictable.
